LCOV - code coverage report
Current view: top level - utils/cache - typcache.c (source / functions) Hit Total Coverage
Test: lcov.info Lines: 190 201 94.5 %
Date: 2017-07-12 15:24:06 Functions: 8 8 100.0 %
Legend: Lines: hit not hit | Branches: + taken - not taken # not executed Branches: 143 184 77.7 % 

           Branch data     Line data    Source code
       1                 :            : /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
       2                 :            :  *
       3                 :            :  * typcache.c
       4                 :            :  *        POSTGRES type cache code
       5                 :            :  *
       6                 :            :  * The type cache exists to speed lookup of certain information about data
       7                 :            :  * types that is not directly available from a type's pg_type row.  For
       8                 :            :  * example, we use a type's default btree opclass, or the default hash
       9                 :            :  * opclass if no btree opclass exists, to determine which operators should
      10                 :            :  * be used for grouping and sorting the type (GROUP BY, ORDER BY ASC/DESC).
      11                 :            :  *
      12                 :            :  * Several seemingly-odd choices have been made to support use of the type
      13                 :            :  * cache by the generic array comparison routines array_eq() and array_cmp().
      14                 :            :  * Because those routines are used as index support operations, they cannot
      15                 :            :  * leak memory.  To allow them to execute efficiently, all information that
      16                 :            :  * either of them would like to re-use across calls is made available in the
      17                 :            :  * type cache.
      18                 :            :  *
      19                 :            :  * Once created, a type cache entry lives as long as the backend does, so
      20                 :            :  * there is no need for a call to release a cache entry.  (For present uses,
      21                 :            :  * it would be okay to flush type cache entries at the ends of transactions,
      22                 :            :  * if we needed to reclaim space.)
      23                 :            :  *
      24                 :            :  * There is presently no provision for clearing out a cache entry if the
      25                 :            :  * stored data becomes obsolete.  (The code will work if a type acquires
      26                 :            :  * opclasses it didn't have before while a backend runs --- but not if the
      27                 :            :  * definition of an existing opclass is altered.)  However, the relcache
      28                 :            :  * doesn't cope with opclasses changing under it, either, so this seems
      29                 :            :  * a low-priority problem.
      30                 :            :  *
      31                 :            :  * We do support clearing the tuple descriptor part of a rowtype's cache
      32                 :            :  * entry, since that may need to change as a consequence of ALTER TABLE.
      33                 :            :  *
      34                 :            :  *
      35                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2008, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
      36                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
      37                 :            :  *
      38                 :            :  * IDENTIFICATION
      39                 :            :  *        $PostgreSQL: pgsql/src/backend/utils/cache/typcache.c,v 1.27.2.1 2010/09/02 03:17:06 tgl Exp $
      40                 :            :  *
      41                 :            :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
      42                 :            :  */
      43                 :            : #include "postgres.h"
      44                 :            : 
      45                 :            : #include "access/hash.h"
      46                 :            : #include "access/heapam.h"
      47                 :            : #include "access/nbtree.h"
      48                 :            : #include "catalog/pg_type.h"
      49                 :            : #include "commands/defrem.h"
      50                 :            : #include "utils/builtins.h"
      51                 :            : #include "utils/inval.h"
      52                 :            : #include "utils/lsyscache.h"
      53                 :            : #include "utils/syscache.h"
      54                 :            : #include "utils/typcache.h"
      55                 :            : #include "cdb/cdbvars.h"
      56                 :            : 
      57                 :            : 
      58                 :            : /* The main type cache hashtable searched by lookup_type_cache */
      59                 :            : static HTAB *TypeCacheHash = NULL;
      60                 :            : 
      61                 :            : /*
      62                 :            :  * We use a separate table for storing the definitions of non-anonymous
      63                 :            :  * record types.  Once defined, a record type will be remembered for the
      64                 :            :  * life of the backend.  Subsequent uses of the "same" record type (where
      65                 :            :  * sameness means equalTupleDescs) will refer to the existing table entry.
      66                 :            :  *
      67                 :            :  * Stored record types are remembered in a linear array of TupleDescs,
      68                 :            :  * which can be indexed quickly with the assigned typmod.  There is also
      69                 :            :  * a hash table to speed searches for matching TupleDescs.      The hash key
      70                 :            :  * uses just the first N columns' type OIDs, and so we may have multiple
      71                 :            :  * entries with the same hash key.
      72                 :            :  */
      73                 :            : #define REC_HASH_KEYS   16              /* use this many columns in hash key */
      74                 :            : 
      75                 :            : typedef struct RecordCacheEntry
      76                 :            : {
      77                 :            :         /* the hash lookup key MUST BE FIRST */
      78                 :            :         Oid                     hashkey[REC_HASH_KEYS]; /* column type IDs, zero-filled */
      79                 :            : 
      80                 :            :         /* list of TupleDescs for record types with this hashkey */
      81                 :            :         List       *tupdescs;
      82                 :            : } RecordCacheEntry;
      83                 :            : 
      84                 :            : static HTAB *RecordCacheHash = NULL;
      85                 :            : 
      86                 :            : static TupleDesc *RecordCacheArray = NULL;
      87                 :            : static int32 RecordCacheArrayLen = 0;   /* allocated length of array */
      88                 :            : int32 NextRecordTypmod = 0;             /* number of entries used */
      89                 :            : 
      90                 :            : static void TypeCacheRelCallback(Datum arg, Oid relid);
      91                 :            : 
      92                 :            : 
      93                 :            : /*
      94                 :            :  * lookup_type_cache
      95                 :            :  *
      96                 :            :  * Fetch the type cache entry for the specified datatype, and make sure that
      97                 :            :  * all the fields requested by bits in 'flags' are valid.
      98                 :            :  *
      99                 :            :  * The result is never NULL --- we will elog() if the passed type OID is
     100                 :            :  * invalid.  Note however that we may fail to find one or more of the
     101                 :            :  * requested opclass-dependent fields; the caller needs to check whether
     102                 :            :  * the fields are InvalidOid or not.
     103                 :            :  */
     104                 :            : TypeCacheEntry *
     105                 :            : lookup_type_cache(Oid type_id, int flags)
     106                 :            : {
     107                 :            :         TypeCacheEntry *typentry;
     108                 :            :         bool            found;
     109                 :            : 
     110         [ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if (TypeCacheHash == NULL)
     111                 :            :         {
     112                 :            :                 /* First time through: initialize the hash table */
     113                 :            :                 HASHCTL         ctl;
     114                 :            : 
     115         [ -  + ]:       2298 :                 if (!CacheMemoryContext)
     116                 :          0 :                         CreateCacheMemoryContext();
     117                 :            : 
     118 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:      32172 :                 MemSet(&ctl, 0, sizeof(ctl));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
     119                 :       2298 :                 ctl.keysize = sizeof(Oid);
     120                 :       2298 :                 ctl.entrysize = sizeof(TypeCacheEntry);
     121                 :       2298 :                 ctl.hash = oid_hash;
     122                 :       2298 :                 TypeCacheHash = hash_create("Type information cache", 64,
     123                 :            :                                                                         &ctl, HASH_ELEM | HASH_FUNCTION);
     124                 :            : 
     125                 :            :                 /* Also set up a callback for relcache SI invalidations */
     126                 :       2298 :                 CacheRegisterRelcacheCallback(TypeCacheRelCallback, (Datum) 0);
     127                 :       2298 :         }
     128                 :            : 
     129                 :            :         /* Try to look up an existing entry */
     130                 :    2307778 :         typentry = (TypeCacheEntry *) hash_search(TypeCacheHash,
     131                 :    2307778 :                                                                                           (void *) &type_id,
     132                 :            :                                                                                           HASH_FIND, NULL);
     133         [ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if (typentry == NULL)
     134                 :            :         {
     135                 :            :                 /*
     136                 :            :                  * If we didn't find one, we want to make one.  But first look up the
     137                 :            :                  * pg_type row, just to make sure we don't make a cache entry for an
     138                 :            :                  * invalid type OID.
     139                 :            :                  */
     140                 :            :                 HeapTuple       tp;
     141                 :            :                 Form_pg_type typtup;
     142                 :            : 
     143                 :       6910 :                 tp = SearchSysCache(TYPEOID,
     144                 :       6910 :                                                         ObjectIdGetDatum(type_id),
     145                 :            :                                                         0, 0, 0);
     146         [ -  + ]:       6910 :                 if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tp))
     147                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", type_id);
     148                 :       6910 :                 typtup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tp);
     149         [ -  + ]:       6910 :                 if (!typtup->typisdefined)
     150         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
     151                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
     152                 :            :                                          errmsg("type \"%s\" is only a shell",
     153                 :            :                                                         NameStr(typtup->typname))));
     154                 :            : 
     155                 :            :                 /* Now make the typcache entry */
     156                 :       6910 :                 typentry = (TypeCacheEntry *) hash_search(TypeCacheHash,
     157                 :       6910 :                                                                                                   (void *) &type_id,
     158                 :            :                                                                                                   HASH_ENTER, &found);
     159 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       6910 :                 Assert(!found);                 /* it wasn't there a moment ago */
     160                 :            : 
     161 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     138200 :                 MemSet(typentry, 0, sizeof(TypeCacheEntry));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
     162                 :       6910 :                 typentry->type_id = type_id;
     163                 :       6910 :                 typentry->typlen = typtup->typlen;
     164                 :       6910 :                 typentry->typbyval = typtup->typbyval;
     165                 :       6910 :                 typentry->typalign = typtup->typalign;
     166                 :       6910 :                 typentry->typtype = typtup->typtype;
     167                 :       6910 :                 typentry->typrelid = typtup->typrelid;
     168                 :            : 
     169                 :       6910 :                 ReleaseSysCache(tp);
     170                 :       6910 :         }
     171                 :            : 
     172                 :            :         /* If we haven't already found the opclass, try to do so */
     173         [ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & (TYPECACHE_EQ_OPR | TYPECACHE_LT_OPR | TYPECACHE_GT_OPR |
     174                 :            :                                   TYPECACHE_CMP_PROC |
     175                 :            :                                   TYPECACHE_EQ_OPR_FINFO | TYPECACHE_CMP_PROC_FINFO |
     176         [ +  + ]:    2307778 :                                   TYPECACHE_BTREE_OPFAMILY)) &&
     177                 :    1803236 :                 typentry->btree_opf == InvalidOid)
     178                 :            :         {
     179                 :            :                 Oid                     opclass;
     180                 :            : 
     181                 :      70586 :                 opclass = GetDefaultOpClass(type_id, BTREE_AM_OID);
     182         [ +  + ]:      70586 :                 if (OidIsValid(opclass))
     183                 :            :                 {
     184                 :       4303 :                         typentry->btree_opf = get_opclass_family(opclass);
     185                 :       4303 :                         typentry->btree_opintype = get_opclass_input_type(opclass);
     186                 :       4303 :                 }
     187                 :            :                 /* Only care about hash opclass if no btree opclass... */
     188         [ +  + ]:      70586 :                 if (typentry->btree_opf == InvalidOid)
     189                 :            :                 {
     190         [ +  + ]:      66283 :                         if (typentry->hash_opf == InvalidOid)
     191                 :            :                         {
     192                 :       5714 :                                 opclass = GetDefaultOpClass(type_id, HASH_AM_OID);
     193         [ +  + ]:       5714 :                                 if (OidIsValid(opclass))
     194                 :            :                                 {
     195                 :        548 :                                         typentry->hash_opf = get_opclass_family(opclass);
     196                 :        548 :                                         typentry->hash_opintype = get_opclass_input_type(opclass);
     197                 :        548 :                                 }
     198                 :       5714 :                         }
     199                 :      66283 :                 }
     200                 :            :                 else
     201                 :            :                 {
     202                 :            :                         /*
     203                 :            :                          * If we find a btree opclass where previously we only found a
     204                 :            :                          * hash opclass, forget the hash equality operator so we can use
     205                 :            :                          * the btree operator instead.
     206                 :            :                          */
     207                 :       4303 :                         typentry->eq_opr = InvalidOid;
     208                 :       4303 :                         typentry->eq_opr_finfo.fn_oid = InvalidOid;
     209                 :            :                 }
     210                 :      70586 :         }
     211                 :            : 
     212                 :            :         /* Look for requested operators and functions */
     213 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & (TYPECACHE_EQ_OPR | TYPECACHE_EQ_OPR_FINFO)) &&
     214                 :    1703965 :                 typentry->eq_opr == InvalidOid)
     215                 :            :         {
     216         [ +  + ]:       9086 :                 if (typentry->btree_opf != InvalidOid)
     217                 :       3386 :                         typentry->eq_opr = get_opfamily_member(typentry->btree_opf,
     218                 :       3386 :                                                                                                    typentry->btree_opintype,
     219                 :       3386 :                                                                                                    typentry->btree_opintype,
     220                 :            :                                                                                                    BTEqualStrategyNumber);
     221 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:       9086 :                 if (typentry->eq_opr == InvalidOid &&
     222                 :       5700 :                         typentry->hash_opf != InvalidOid)
     223                 :        548 :                         typentry->eq_opr = get_opfamily_member(typentry->hash_opf,
     224                 :        548 :                                                                                                    typentry->hash_opintype,
     225                 :        548 :                                                                                                    typentry->hash_opintype,
     226                 :            :                                                                                                    HTEqualStrategyNumber);
     227                 :       9086 :         }
     228 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & TYPECACHE_LT_OPR) && typentry->lt_opr == InvalidOid)
     229                 :            :         {
     230         [ +  + ]:      68853 :                 if (typentry->btree_opf != InvalidOid)
     231                 :       3281 :                         typentry->lt_opr = get_opfamily_member(typentry->btree_opf,
     232                 :       3281 :                                                                                                    typentry->btree_opintype,
     233                 :       3281 :                                                                                                    typentry->btree_opintype,
     234                 :            :                                                                                                    BTLessStrategyNumber);
     235                 :      68853 :         }
     236 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & TYPECACHE_GT_OPR) && typentry->gt_opr == InvalidOid)
     237                 :            :         {
     238         [ +  + ]:      67784 :                 if (typentry->btree_opf != InvalidOid)
     239                 :       2258 :                         typentry->gt_opr = get_opfamily_member(typentry->btree_opf,
     240                 :       2258 :                                                                                                    typentry->btree_opintype,
     241                 :       2258 :                                                                                                    typentry->btree_opintype,
     242                 :            :                                                                                                    BTGreaterStrategyNumber);
     243                 :      67784 :         }
     244 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & (TYPECACHE_CMP_PROC | TYPECACHE_CMP_PROC_FINFO)) &&
     245                 :     120454 :                 typentry->cmp_proc == InvalidOid)
     246                 :            :         {
     247         [ +  + ]:      67342 :                 if (typentry->btree_opf != InvalidOid)
     248                 :       1821 :                         typentry->cmp_proc = get_opfamily_proc(typentry->btree_opf,

     250                 :       1821 :                                                                                                    typentry->btree_opintype,
     251                 :            :                                                                                                    BTORDER_PROC);
     252                 :      67342 :         }
     253                 :            : 
     254                 :            :         /*
     255                 :            :          * Set up fmgr lookup info as requested
     256                 :            :          *
     257                 :            :          * Note: we tell fmgr the finfo structures live in CacheMemoryContext,
     258                 :            :          * which is not quite right (they're really in DynaHashContext) but this
     259                 :            :          * will do for our purposes.
     260                 :            :          */
     261 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & TYPECACHE_EQ_OPR_FINFO) &&
     262         [ +  + ]:     120450 :                 typentry->eq_opr_finfo.fn_oid == InvalidOid &&
     263                 :       6842 :                 typentry->eq_opr != InvalidOid)
     264                 :            :         {
     265                 :            :                 Oid                     eq_opr_func;
     266                 :            : 
     267                 :       2338 :                 eq_opr_func = get_opcode(typentry->eq_opr);
     268         [ +  - ]:       2338 :                 if (eq_opr_func != InvalidOid)
     269                 :       2338 :                         fmgr_info_cxt(eq_opr_func, &typentry->eq_opr_finfo,
     270                 :       2338 :                                                   CacheMemoryContext);
     271                 :       2338 :         }
     272 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & TYPECACHE_CMP_PROC_FINFO) &&
     273         [ +  + ]:     120392 :                 typentry->cmp_proc_finfo.fn_oid == InvalidOid &&
     274                 :      67330 :                 typentry->cmp_proc != InvalidOid)
     275                 :            :         {
     276                 :       1803 :                 fmgr_info_cxt(typentry->cmp_proc, &typentry->cmp_proc_finfo,
     277                 :       1803 :                                           CacheMemoryContext);
     278                 :       1803 :         }
     279                 :            : 
     280                 :            :         /*
     281                 :            :          * If it's a composite type (row type), get tupdesc if requested
     282                 :            :          */
     283 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    2307778 :         if ((flags & TYPECACHE_TUPDESC) &&
     284         [ +  + ]:     624614 :                 typentry->tupDesc == NULL &&
     285                 :     597973 :                 typentry->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE)
     286                 :            :         {
     287                 :            :                 Relation        rel;
     288                 :            : 
     289         [ -  + ]:        188 :                 if (!OidIsValid(typentry->typrelid)) /* should not happen */
     290                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "invalid typrelid for composite type %u",
     291                 :            :                                  typentry->type_id);
     292                 :        188 :                 rel = relation_open(typentry->typrelid, AccessShareLock);
     293 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        188 :                 Assert(rel->rd_rel->reltype == typentry->type_id);
     294                 :            : 
     295                 :            :                 /*
     296                 :            :                  * Link to the tupdesc and increment its refcount (we assert it's a
     297                 :            :                  * refcounted descriptor).      We don't use IncrTupleDescRefCount() for
     298                 :            :                  * this, because the reference mustn't be entered in the current
     299                 :            :                  * resource owner; it can outlive the current query.
     300                 :            :                  */
     301                 :        188 :                 typentry->tupDesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
     302                 :            : 
     303 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        188 :                 Assert(typentry->tupDesc->tdrefcount > 0);
     304                 :        188 :                 typentry->tupDesc->tdrefcount++;
     305                 :            : 
     306                 :        188 :                 relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
     307                 :        188 :         }
     308                 :            : 
     309                 :    2307778 :         return typentry;
     310                 :            : }
     311                 :            : 
     312                 :            : /*
     313                 :            :  * lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_internal --- internal routine to lookup a rowtype
     314                 :            :  *
     315                 :            :  * Same API as lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_noerror, but the returned tupdesc
     316                 :            :  * hasn't had its refcount bumped.
     317                 :            :  */
     318                 :            : static TupleDesc
     319                 :            : lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_internal(Oid type_id, int32 typmod, bool noError)
     320                 :            : {
     321         [ +  + ]:     706991 :         if (type_id != RECORDOID)
     322                 :            :         {
     323                 :            :                 /*
     324                 :            :                  * It's a named composite type, so use the regular typcache.
     325                 :            :                  */
     326                 :            :                 TypeCacheEntry *typentry;
     327                 :            : 
     328                 :     504542 :                 typentry = lookup_type_cache(type_id, TYPECACHE_TUPDESC);
     329 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:     504542 :                 if (typentry->tupDesc == NULL && !noError)
     330         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
     331                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
     332                 :            :                                          errmsg("type %s is not composite",
     333                 :            :                                                         format_type_be(type_id))));
     334                 :     504542 :                 return typentry->tupDesc;
     335                 :            :         }
     336                 :            :         else
     337                 :            :         {
     338                 :            :                 /*
     339                 :            :                  * It's a transient record type, so look in our record-type table.
     340                 :            :                  */
     341 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:     202449 :                 if (typmod < 0 || typmod >= NextRecordTypmod)
     342                 :            :                 {
     343         [ +  + ]:     200952 :                         if (!noError)
     344         [ +  - ]:          1 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
     345                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
     346                 :            :                                                  errmsg("record type has not been registered")));
     347                 :     200951 :                         return NULL;
     348                 :            :                 }
     349                 :       1497 :                 return RecordCacheArray[typmod];
     350                 :            :         }
     351                 :     706990 : }
     352                 :            : 
     353                 :            : /*
     354                 :            :  * lookup_rowtype_tupdesc
     355                 :            :  *
     356                 :            :  * Given a typeid/typmod that should describe a known composite type,
     357                 :            :  * return the tuple descriptor for the type.  Will ereport on failure.
     358                 :            :  *
     359                 :            :  * Note: on success, we increment the refcount of the returned TupleDesc,
     360                 :            :  * and log the reference in CurrentResourceOwner.  Caller should call
     361                 :            :  * ReleaseTupleDesc or DecrTupleDescRefCount when done using the tupdesc.
     362                 :            :  */
     363                 :            : TupleDesc
     364                 :            : lookup_rowtype_tupdesc(Oid type_id, int32 typmod)
     365                 :            : {
     366                 :            :         TupleDesc       tupDesc;
     367                 :            : 
     368                 :       1679 :         tupDesc = lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_internal(type_id, typmod, false);
     369                 :       1679 :         IncrTupleDescRefCount(tupDesc);
     370                 :       1679 :         return tupDesc;
     371                 :            : }
     372                 :            : 
     373                 :            : /*
     374                 :            :  * lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_noerror
     375                 :            :  *
     376                 :            :  * As above, but if the type is not a known composite type and noError
     377                 :            :  * is true, returns NULL instead of ereport'ing.  (Note that if a bogus
     378                 :            :  * type_id is passed, you'll get an ereport anyway.)
     379                 :            :  */
     380                 :            : TupleDesc
     381                 :            : lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_noerror(Oid type_id, int32 typmod, bool noError)
     382                 :            : {
     383                 :            :         TupleDesc       tupDesc;
     384                 :            : 
     385                 :     696987 :         tupDesc = lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_internal(type_id, typmod, noError);
     386         [ +  + ]:     696987 :         if (tupDesc != NULL)
     387                 :      18205 :                 IncrTupleDescRefCount(tupDesc);
     388                 :     696987 :         return tupDesc;
     389                 :            : }
     390                 :            : 
     391                 :            : /*
     392                 :            :  * lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_copy
     393                 :            :  *
     394                 :            :  * Like lookup_rowtype_tupdesc(), but the returned TupleDesc has been
     395                 :            :  * copied into the CurrentMemoryContext and is not reference-counted.
     396                 :            :  */
     397                 :            : TupleDesc
     398                 :            : lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_copy(Oid type_id, int32 typmod)
     399                 :            : {
     400                 :            :         TupleDesc       tmp;
     401                 :            : 
     402                 :       8325 :         tmp = lookup_rowtype_tupdesc_internal(type_id, typmod, false);
     403                 :       8325 :         return CreateTupleDescCopyConstr(tmp);
     404                 :            : }
     405                 :            : 
     406                 :            : 
     407                 :            : /*
     408                 :            :  * assign_record_type_typmod
     409                 :            :  *
     410                 :            :  * Given a tuple descriptor for a RECORD type, find or create a cache entry
     411                 :            :  * for the type, and set the tupdesc's tdtypmod field to a value that will
     412                 :            :  * identify this cache entry to lookup_rowtype_tupdesc.
     413                 :            :  */
     414                 :            : void
     415                 :            : assign_record_type_typmod(TupleDesc tupDesc)
     416                 :            : {
     417                 :            :         RecordCacheEntry *recentry;
     418                 :            :         TupleDesc       entDesc;
     419                 :            :         Oid                     hashkey[REC_HASH_KEYS];
     420                 :            :         bool            found;
     421                 :            :         int                     i;
     422                 :            :         ListCell   *l;
     423                 :            :         int32           newtypmod;
     424                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     425                 :            : 
     426 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      10165 :         Assert(tupDesc->tdtypeid == RECORDOID);
     427                 :            : 
     428         [ +  + ]:      10165 :         if (RecordCacheHash == NULL)
     429                 :            :         {
     430                 :            :                 /* First time through: initialize the hash table */
     431                 :            :                 HASHCTL         ctl;
     432                 :            : 
     433         [ -  + ]:        574 :                 if (!CacheMemoryContext)
     434                 :          0 :                         CreateCacheMemoryContext();
     435                 :            : 
     436 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:       8036 :                 MemSet(&ctl, 0, sizeof(ctl));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
     437                 :        574 :                 ctl.keysize = REC_HASH_KEYS * sizeof(Oid);
     438                 :        574 :                 ctl.entrysize = sizeof(RecordCacheEntry);
     439                 :        574 :                 ctl.hash = tag_hash;
     440                 :        574 :                 RecordCacheHash = hash_create("Record information cache", 64,
     441                 :            :                                                                           &ctl, HASH_ELEM | HASH_FUNCTION);
     442                 :        574 :         }
     443                 :            : 
     444                 :            :         /* Find or create a hashtable entry for this hash class */
     445 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:      91485 :         MemSet(hashkey, 0, sizeof(hashkey));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
     446         [ +  + ]:      56307 :         for (i = 0; i < tupDesc->natts; i++)
     447                 :            :         {
     448         [ +  + ]:      46408 :                 if (i >= REC_HASH_KEYS)
     449                 :        266 :                         break;
     450                 :      46142 :                 hashkey[i] = tupDesc->attrs[i]->atttypid;
     451                 :      46142 :         }
     452                 :      10165 :         recentry = (RecordCacheEntry *) hash_search(RecordCacheHash,
     453                 :      10165 :                                                                                                 (void *) hashkey,
     454                 :            :                                                                                                 HASH_ENTER, &found);
     455         [ +  + ]:      10165 :         if (!found)
     456                 :            :         {
     457                 :            :                 /* New entry ... hash_search initialized only the hash key */
     458                 :        853 :                 recentry->tupdescs = NIL;
     459                 :        853 :         }
     460                 :            : 
     461                 :            :         /* Look for existing record cache entry */
     462         [ +  + ]:      14757 :         foreach(l, recentry->tupdescs)
     463                 :            :         {
     464                 :      13673 :                 entDesc = (TupleDesc) lfirst(l);
     465         [ +  + ]:      13673 :                 if (equalTupleDescs(tupDesc, entDesc, true))
     466                 :            :                 {
     467                 :       9081 :                         tupDesc->tdtypmod = entDesc->tdtypmod;
     468                 :            : 
     469                 :       9081 :                         return;
     470                 :            :                 }
     471                 :       4592 :         }
     472                 :            : 
     473                 :            :         /* Not present, so need to manufacture an entry */
     474                 :       1084 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
     475                 :            : 
     476         [ +  + ]:       1084 :         if (RecordCacheArray == NULL)
     477                 :            :         {
     478                 :        574 :                 RecordCacheArray = (TupleDesc *) palloc(64 * sizeof(TupleDesc));
     479                 :        574 :                 RecordCacheArrayLen = 64;
     480                 :        574 :         }
     481         [ -  + ]:        510 :         else if (NextRecordTypmod >= RecordCacheArrayLen)
     482                 :            :         {
     483                 :          0 :                 int32           newlen = RecordCacheArrayLen * 2;
     484                 :            : 
     485                 :          0 :                 RecordCacheArray = (TupleDesc *) repalloc(RecordCacheArray,
     486                 :            :                                                                                                   newlen * sizeof(TupleDesc));
     487                 :          0 :                 RecordCacheArrayLen = newlen;
     488                 :          0 :         }
     489                 :            : 
     490                 :            :         /* if fail in subrs, no damage except possibly some wasted memory... */
     491                 :       1084 :         entDesc = CreateTupleDescCopy(tupDesc);
     492                 :       1084 :         recentry->tupdescs = lcons(entDesc, recentry->tupdescs);
     493                 :            :         /* mark it as a reference-counted tupdesc */
     494                 :       1084 :         entDesc->tdrefcount = 1;
     495                 :            :         /* now it's safe to advance NextRecordTypmod */
     496                 :       1084 :         newtypmod = NextRecordTypmod++;
     497                 :       1084 :         entDesc->tdtypmod = newtypmod;
     498                 :       1084 :         RecordCacheArray[newtypmod] = entDesc;
     499                 :            : 
     500                 :            :         /* report to caller as well */
     501                 :       1084 :         tupDesc->tdtypmod = newtypmod;
     502                 :            : 
     503                 :       1084 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     504                 :      10165 : }
     505                 :            : 
     506                 :            : /*
     507                 :            :  * TypeCacheRelCallback
     508                 :            :  *              Relcache inval callback function
     509                 :            :  *
     510                 :            :  * Delete the cached tuple descriptor (if any) for the given rel's composite
     511                 :            :  * type, or for all composite types if relid == InvalidOid.
     512                 :            :  *
     513                 :            :  * This is called when a relcache invalidation event occurs for the given
     514                 :            :  * relid.  We must scan the whole typcache hash since we don't know the
     515                 :            :  * type OID corresponding to the relid.  We could do a direct search if this
     516                 :            :  * were a syscache-flush callback on pg_type, but then we would need all
     517                 :            :  * ALTER-TABLE-like commands that could modify a rowtype to issue syscache
     518                 :            :  * invals against the rel's pg_type OID.  The extra SI signaling could very
     519                 :            :  * well cost more than we'd save, since in most usages there are not very
     520                 :            :  * many entries in a backend's typcache.  The risk of bugs-of-omission seems
     521                 :            :  * high, too.
     522                 :            :  *
     523                 :            :  * Another possibility, with only localized impact, is to maintain a second
     524                 :            :  * hashtable that indexes composite-type typcache entries by their typrelid.
     525                 :            :  * But it's still not clear it's worth the trouble.
     526                 :            :  */
     527                 :            : static void
     528                 :            : TypeCacheRelCallback(Datum arg, Oid relid)
     529                 :            : {
     530                 :            :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
     531                 :            :         TypeCacheEntry *typentry;
     532                 :            : 
     533                 :            :         /* TypeCacheHash must exist, else this callback wouldn't be registered */
     534                 :    2174927 :         hash_seq_init(&status, TypeCacheHash);
     535         [ +  + ]:   11950318 :         while ((typentry = (TypeCacheEntry *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
     536                 :            :         {
     537         [ +  + ]:    9775391 :                 if (typentry->tupDesc == NULL)
     538                 :    9712763 :                         continue;       /* not composite, or tupdesc hasn't been requested */
     539                 :            : 
     540                 :            :                 /* Delete if match, or if we're zapping all composite types */
     541 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:      62628 :                 if (relid == typentry->typrelid || relid == InvalidOid)
     542                 :            :                 {
     543                 :            :                         /*
     544                 :            :                          * Release our refcount, and free the tupdesc if none remain.
     545                 :            :                          * (Can't use DecrTupleDescRefCount because this reference is not
     546                 :            :                          * logged in current resource owner.)
     547                 :            :                          */
     548 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         99 :                         Assert(typentry->tupDesc->tdrefcount > 0);
     549         [ +  + ]:         99 :                         if (--typentry->tupDesc->tdrefcount == 0)
     550                 :         98 :                                 FreeTupleDesc(typentry->tupDesc);
     551                 :         99 :                         typentry->tupDesc = NULL;
     552                 :         99 :                 }
     553                 :            :         }
     554                 :    2174927 : }
     555                 :            : 
     556                 :            : 
     557                 :            : /*
     558                 :            :  * build_tuple_node_list
     559                 :            :  *
     560                 :            :  * Wrap TupleDesc with TupleDescNode. Return all record type in record cache.
     561                 :            :  */
     562                 :            : List *
     563                 :            : build_tuple_node_list(int start)
     564                 :            : {
     565                 :        165 :         List *transientTypeList = NIL;
     566                 :        165 :         int i = start;
     567                 :            : 
     568         [ -  + ]:        165 :         if (NextRecordTypmod == 0)
     569                 :          0 :                 return transientTypeList;
     570                 :            : 
     571         [ +  + ]:        861 :         for (; i < NextRecordTypmod; i++)
     572                 :            :         {
     573                 :        696 :                 TupleDesc tmp = RecordCacheArray[i];
     574                 :            : 
     575                 :        696 :                 TupleDescNode *node = palloc0(sizeof(TupleDescNode));
     576                 :        696 :                 node->type = T_TupleDescNode;
     577                 :        696 :                 node->natts = tmp->natts;
     578                 :        696 :                 node->tuple = CreateTupleDescCopy(tmp);
     579                 :        696 :                 transientTypeList = lappend(transientTypeList, node);
     580                 :        696 :         }
     581                 :            : 
     582                 :        165 :         return transientTypeList;
     583                 :        165 : }

Generated by: LCOV version 1.13
